Breast Cancer

Sources: Open Targets, HPO, ClinicalTrials.gov, DGIdb, ChEMBL, PubMed Literature, PubMed NP, ClinicalTrials.gov NP · Pipeline phase 7 · Updated 2026-07-05

Remission & chronicity

Spontaneous remission
Spontaneous regression documented but rare (<0.5%); 5-year survival stage I: ~99%; stage IV: ~28% overall, improving with modern therapy
Best-intervention remission
HER2+ metastatic: pertuzumab + trastuzumab + docetaxel (CLEOPATRA: 57.1-month median OS vs 40.8 months); HR+/HER2- with CDK4/6 inhibitors: 63-month PFS in first-line (MONALEESA-2); TNBC with pembrolizumab + chemotherapy: 23-month OS vs 16.1 months; pCR with neoadjuvant: 80-90% in HER2+ with dual HER2 blockade
Drug-free remission
—
Relapse after remission
—
Chronicity
—
Gap size
Varies greatly by subtype; HER2+ and HR+ have dramatically improved outcomes; TNBC lags; metastatic remains incurable in most patients
Primary barrier
biological (metastatic) + access (treatment disparities by race/SES)
Barrier detail: Racial disparities in breast cancer mortality persist (Black women 40% higher mortality despite similar incidence); access to genetic testing (BRCA1/2) and CDK4/6 inhibitors varies significantly; germline testing vastly underutilised

Last SoC change: 2023 — antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) transforming TNBC (sacituzumab govitecan 2020, trastuzumab deruxtecan for HER2-low 2022); olaparib adjuvant in BRCA-mutated HER2- (OlympiA 2021); CDK4/6 inhibitors first-line HR+/HER2- since 2015; trastuzumab HER2+ since 1998

Remission data layers: disease_db_100 · source-locked manual data · confidence: high

Identifiers: MONDO: MONDO:0007254 · ORPHA: ORPHA:227535

Therapeutics

Direct disease associations, biomarker-linked drugs, OSMF narrative-review natural agents, and merged ranking. Only agents with registry trials or published clinical literature are shown by default.

DrugTypePhaseEvidence ScorePrice (UK / US)Trials & literatureSourcesLinks
BICALUTAMIDE Small molecule Approved Strong 105 Varies — check openprescribing.net (UK) or costplusdrugs.com / GoodRx (US)
8 trials 3 Trial pubs
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT02605486 — Palbociclib in Combination With Bicalutamide for the Treatment of AR(+) Metastatic Breast Cancer (MBC) (ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING) PHASE1, PHASE2
  • NCT05095207 — Abemaciclib in Combination With Bicalutamide for Androgen Receptor-positive, HER2-negative Metastatic Breast Cancer (RECRUITING) PHASE1, PHASE2
  • NCT06365788 — Bicalutamide and Abemaciclib in Inoperable or Metastatic Androgen Receptor-positive Triple-negative Breast Cancer (RECRUITING) PHASE2
  • NCT03650894 — Nivolumab, Ipilimumab, and Bicalutamide in Human Epidermal Growth Factor (HER) 2 Negative Breast Cancer Patients (ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING) PHASE2
  • NCT00590213 — Compare the Value of Prophylactic Versus Therapeutic Breast Radiotherapy in CASODEX (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00468715 — Bicalutamide in Treating Patients With Metastatic Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT06727357 — Next Generation Sequencing-based "Oncochip" for Therapeutic Decision in Metastatic Breast Cancer. Study SHARP (ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING) PHASE2
  • NCT03055312 — Bicalutamide in Treatment of Androgen Receptor (AR) Positive Metastatic Triple Negative Breast Cancer (TERMINATED) PHASE3

Published literature

  • Clinical trial publication Serial [(18)F]-FDHT-PET to predict bicalutamide efficacy in patients with androgen receptor positive metastatic breast cancer (European journal of cancer (Oxford, England : 1990), 2021)
  • Clinical trial publication Bicalutamide plus Aromatase Inhibitor in Patients with Estrogen Receptor-Positive/Androgen Receptor-Positive Advanced Breast Cancer (The oncologist, 2020)
  • Clinical trial publication Phase II trial of bicalutamide in patients with androgen receptor-positive, estrogen receptor-negative metastatic Breast Cancer (Clinical cancer research : an official journal of the American Association for Cancer Research, 2013)

EPIRUBICIN Small molecule Approved Strong 105 Hospital / IV specialty — prices per vial or dose (often hundreds–thousands USD/GBP)
8 trials 11 Cochrane 21 Meta-analysis 975 Trial pubs 561 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT00003577 — Combination Chemotherapy With or Without Epirubicin in Treating Women With Stage I or Stage II Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT02067416 — PREDATOR: Neoadjuvant Gene Prediction for Breast Cancer (WITHDRAWN) PHASE2
  • NCT02115152 — Neoadjuvant Trial of Capecitabine for Axillary Lymph Node Positive Operable Breast Cancer (UNKNOWN) PHASE2
  • NCT01036087 — Panitumumab, Nab-paclitaxel and Carboplatin for HER2 Negative Inflammatory Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT05909332 — Study of Antivascular Therapy Combined With Chemotherapy Versus Chemotherapy in Adjuvant Therapy of TNBC-BLIS Patients (BCTOP-T-A03) (RECRUITING) PHASE3
  • NCT01996267 — Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy in HER2 Positive Breast Cancer, TRAIN-2 (ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING) PHASE3
  • NCT00003012 — Combination Chemotherapy Following Surgery in Treating Women With Early Stage Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT01159236 — Molecular Triaging of Newly Diagnosed Breast Cancer (WITHDRAWN) NA

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Efficacy and cardiovascular safety of liposomal doxorubicin: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized trials (Cardio-oncology (London, England), 2025)
  • Cochrane review Comparison of the efficacy and safety of docetaxel plus capecitabine versus docetaxel plus epirubicin for human epidermal growth factor 2 -negative breast cancer: a meta-analysis (BMC women's health, 2025)
  • Cochrane review The Omission of Anthracycline Chemotherapy in Women with Early HER2-Negative Breast Cancer-A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis (Current oncology (Toronto, Ont.), 2024)
  • Cochrane review Concurrent epirubicin and trastuzumab use increases complete pathological response rate without additional cardiotoxicity in patients with human epidermal growth factor receptor 2-positive early breast cancer: A meta-regression analysis (Cancer medicine, 2024)
  • Cochrane review Effects of Integrated Chinese Traditional Medicine and Conventional Western Medicine on the Quality of Life of Breast Cancer Patients: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis (Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis Sequential vs concurrent adjuvant chemotherapy of anthracycline and taxane for operable breast cancer (World journal of surgical oncology, 2021)
  • Meta-analysis Pharmacological interventions for preventing anthracycline-induced clinical and subclinical cardiotoxicity: A network meta-analysis of metastatic breast cancer (Journal of oncology pharmacy practice : official publication of the International Society of Oncology Pharmacy Practitioners, 2021)
  • Systematic review [Effect of taste perception on nutritional status in patients with breast cancer: a systematic review] (Nutricion hospitalaria, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Pragmatic Clinical Trial Assessing Response to Neoadjuvant Docetaxel and Trastuzumab in Nigerian Women With Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2-Positive Breast Cancer (ARETTA) (JCO global oncology, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Durvalumab in Combination With Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy in Early Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: Long-Term Analysis From the GeparNuevo Trial (Journal of clinical oncology : official journal of the American Society of Clinical Oncology, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Surgical Outcomes After Neoadjuvant Pembrolizumab Plus Chemotherapy for Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: Results from the Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Phase 3 KEYNOTE-522 Study (Annals of surgical oncology, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of adjuvant carboplatin intensified chemotherapy versus standard chemotherapy on survival in women with high risk, early stage, triple negative breast cancer (CITRINE): randomised, open label phase 3 trial (BMJ (Clinical research ed.), 2025)

Registry trials

  • NCT04591431 — The Rome Trial From Histology to Target: the Road to Personalize Target Therapy and Immunotherapy (UNKNOWN) PHASE2
  • NCT03805399 — FUSCC Refractory TNBC Umbrella (FUTURE) (UNKNOWN) PHASE1, PHASE2
  • NCT02520063 — Combination of Letrozole, Everolimus and TRC105 in Postmenopausal Women With Hormone-Receptor Positive and Her2 Negative Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE1, PHASE2
  • NCT01088893 — Everolimus in Breast Cancer Patients After Pre-operative Chemotherapy (UNKNOWN) PHASE2
  • NCT03659136 — The XENERA™ 1 Study Tests Xentuzumab in Combination With Everolimus and Exemestane in Women With Hormone Receptor Positive and HER2-negative Breast Cancer That Has Spread (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT05983107 — Chidamide/Everolimus for PIK3CA Wild-type/Mutant HR+/HER2- Advanced Breast Cancer (RECRUITING) PHASE2

Published literature

  • Systematic review mTOR inhibitors in breast cancer: a systematic review (Gynecologic oncology, 2012)
  • Clinical trial publication Sirolimus and trastuzumab combination therapy for HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer after progression on prior trastuzumab therapy (Breast cancer research and treatment, 2015)
  • Clinical trial publication Phase I, pharmacokinetic study of temsirolimus administered orally to patients with advanced cancer (Investigational new drugs, 2010)

View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT04681911 — Inetetamab Combined With Pyrotinib and Chemotherapy in the Treatment of HER2 Positive Metastatic Breast Cancer (UNKNOWN) PHASE2
  • NCT01133912 — Preoperative Chemotherapy With Paclitaxel, Gemcitabine, and Lapatinib (Tykerb®) (PGT) (COMPLETED) PHASE1
  • NCT00662129 — Paclitaxel Albumin-Stabilized Nanoparticle Formulation, Gemcitabine, and Bevacizumab in Treating Patients With Metastatic Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT00191854 — Gemcitabine Combinations in Metastatic Breast Cancer (MBC), 1st Line (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT02046421 — Carboplatin, Gemcitabine Hydrochloride, and Mifepristone in Treating Patients With Advanced Breast Cancer or Recurrent or Persistent Ovarian Epithelial, Fallopian Tube, or Primary Peritoneal Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE1
  • NCT02139358 — Phase I/IIa Trial of Gemcitabine Plus Trastuzumab and Pertuzumab in Previously Treated Metastatic HER2+ Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE1, PHASE2
  • NCT02544243 — Vinorelbine/Gemcitabine Versus Vinorelbine/Cisplatin in Metastatic Breast Cancer (UNKNOWN) PHASE2
  • NCT06481553 — RWS of Paclitaxel Liposome Combined With Anti-HER-2 Monoclonal Antibody (UNKNOWN)

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Different Chemotherapy Regimens and Pathologic Complete Response in Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: An Updated Network Meta-Analysis of Phase 3 Trials (Medicina (Kaunas, Lithuania), 2024)
  • Cochrane review Radiation recall dermatitis: A review of the literature (Seminars in oncology, 2022)
  • Cochrane review Network meta-analysis of eribulin versus other chemotherapies used as second- or later-line treatment in locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer (BMC cancer, 2021)
  • Cochrane review The Impact of Platinum-Containing Chemotherapies in Advanced Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: Meta-Analytical Approach to Evaluating Its Efficacy and Safety (Oncology research and treatment, 2021)
  • Cochrane review Comparative Effectiveness of Taxane-Containing Regimens for Treatment of HER2-Negative Metastatic Breast Cancer: A Network Meta-analysis (Pharmacotherapy, 2019)
  • Meta-analysis Integration Analysis of Bayesian and Machine Learning for Heterogeneity, Biomarkers, and Optimal Combination Regimens of Pucotenlimab in Solid Tumors (Cancer medicine, 2026)
  • Systematic review Efficacy and safety of palliative chemotherapy for patients with advanced breast cancer pretreated with anthracyclines and taxanes: a systematic review (The Lancet. Oncology, 2011)
  • Systematic review The clinical efficacy of cytotoxic agents in locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer patients pretreated with an anthracycline and a taxane: a systematic review (European journal of cancer (Oxford, England : 1990), 2009)
  • Systematic review Multiple-treatments meta-analysis of chemotherapy and targeted therapies in advanced breast cancer (Journal of the National Cancer Institute, 2008)
  • Systematic review Time to full publication of studies of anti-cancer medicines for breast cancer and the potential for publication bias: a short systematic review (Health technology assessment (Winchester, England), 2008)
  • Clinical trial publication A phase II randomized trial of gemcitabine plus cisplatin (GP) versus gemcitabine plus carboplatin (GC) as the first-line treatment of patients with metastatic triple-negative breast cancer (ESMO open, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Datopotamab deruxtecan versus chemotherapy in previously treated inoperable/metastatic hormone receptor-positive, HER2-negative breast cancer: final overall survival analysis of the phase III TROPION-Breast01 study (Annals of oncology : official journal of the European Society for Medical Oncology, 2026)

4 trials 119 Trial pubs 60 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT01196455 — Capecitabine and Mitomycin C in Treatment of Patients With Metastatic Breast Cancer (UNKNOWN) PHASE2
  • NCT02299999 — SAFIR02_Breast - Efficacy of Genome Analysis as a Therapeutic Decision Tool for Patients With Metastatic Breast Cancer (ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING) PHASE2
  • NCT01091168 — Trial of Vinflunine Versus Alkylating Agent in Metastatic Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00003893 — Adjuvant Chemotherapy Plus Radiation Therapy in Treating Women With Early-Stage Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE3

Published literature

  • Systematic review A rapid and systematic review of the effectiveness and cost-effectiveness of the taxanes used in the treatment of advanced breast and ovarian cancer (Health technology assessment (Winchester, England), 2000)
  • Clinical trial publication Role of Mitomycin C in Preventing Capsular Contracture in Implant-Based Reconstructive Breast Surgery: A Randomized Controlled Trial (Plastic and reconstructive surgery, 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication Mitomycin C in combination with vinorelbine in anthracycline- and/or taxane-pretreated patients with metastatic breast cancer (Onkologie, 2012)
  • Clinical trial publication Low expression levels of ATM may substitute for CHEK2 /TP53 mutations predicting resistance towards anthracycline and mitomycin chemotherapy in breast cancer (Breast cancer research : BCR, 2012)
  • Clinical trial publication Combination of capecitabine and mitomycin C as first-line treatment in patients with metastatic breast cancer (Neoplasma, 2011)
  • Clinical trial publication Transarterial chemoembolization (TACE) with mitomycin C and gemcitabine for liver metastases in breast cancer (European radiology, 2010)
  • Randomized controlled trial Randomized phase II trial on mitomycin-C/cisplatin +/- KLT in heavily pretreated advanced breast cancer (The American journal of Chinese medicine, 2008)
  • Randomized controlled trial Good clinical response of breast cancers to neoadjuvant chemoendocrine therapy is associated with improved overall survival (Annals of oncology : official journal of the European Society for Medical Oncology, 2005)

Published literature

  • Clinical trial publication Mitoxantrone hydrochloride liposome (Lipo-MIT) combined with capecitabine in HER2-negative advanced breast cancer: a dose-escalation, phase I study (BMC medicine, 2026)

  • NCT00590213 — Compare the Value of Prophylactic Versus Therapeutic Breast Radiotherapy in CASODEX (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00468715 — Bicalutamide in Treating Patients With Metastatic Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT06727357 — Next Generation Sequencing-based "Oncochip" for Therapeutic Decision in Metastatic Breast Cancer. Study SHARP (ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING) PHASE2

Registry trials

  • NCT00003577 — Combination Chemotherapy With or Without Epirubicin in Treating Women With Stage I or Stage II Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT02067416 — PREDATOR: Neoadjuvant Gene Prediction for Breast Cancer (WITHDRAWN) PHASE2
  • NCT02115152 — Neoadjuvant Trial of Capecitabine for Axillary Lymph Node Positive Operable Breast Cancer (UNKNOWN) PHASE2
  • NCT01036087 — Panitumumab, Nab-paclitaxel and Carboplatin for HER2 Negative Inflammatory Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT05909332 — Study of Antivascular Therapy Combined With Chemotherapy Versus Chemotherapy in Adjuvant Therapy of TNBC-BLIS Patients (BCTOP-T-A03) (RECRUITING) PHASE3
  • NCT01996267 — Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy in HER2 Positive Breast Cancer, TRAIN-2 (ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING) PHASE3
  • NCT00003012 — Combination Chemotherapy Following Surgery in Treating Women With Early Stage Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT01159236 — Molecular Triaging of Newly Diagnosed Breast Cancer (WITHDRAWN) NA

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Efficacy and cardiovascular safety of liposomal doxorubicin: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized trials (Cardio-oncology (London, England), 2025)
  • Cochrane review Comparison of the efficacy and safety of docetaxel plus capecitabine versus docetaxel plus epirubicin for human epidermal growth factor 2 -negative breast cancer: a meta-analysis (BMC women's health, 2025)
  • Cochrane review The Omission of Anthracycline Chemotherapy in Women with Early HER2-Negative Breast Cancer-A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis (Current oncology (Toronto, Ont.), 2024)
  • Cochrane review Concurrent epirubicin and trastuzumab use increases complete pathological response rate without additional cardiotoxicity in patients with human epidermal growth factor receptor 2-positive early breast cancer: A meta-regression analysis (Cancer medicine, 2024)
  • Cochrane review Effects of Integrated Chinese Traditional Medicine and Conventional Western Medicine on the Quality of Life of Breast Cancer Patients: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis (Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis Sequential vs concurrent adjuvant chemotherapy of anthracycline and taxane for operable breast cancer (World journal of surgical oncology, 2021)
  • Meta-analysis Pharmacological interventions for preventing anthracycline-induced clinical and subclinical cardiotoxicity: A network meta-analysis of metastatic breast cancer (Journal of oncology pharmacy practice : official publication of the International Society of Oncology Pharmacy Practitioners, 2021)
  • Systematic review [Effect of taste perception on nutritional status in patients with breast cancer: a systematic review] (Nutricion hospitalaria, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Pragmatic Clinical Trial Assessing Response to Neoadjuvant Docetaxel and Trastuzumab in Nigerian Women With Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2-Positive Breast Cancer (ARETTA) (JCO global oncology, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Durvalumab in Combination With Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy in Early Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: Long-Term Analysis From the GeparNuevo Trial (Journal of clinical oncology : official journal of the American Society of Clinical Oncology, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Surgical Outcomes After Neoadjuvant Pembrolizumab Plus Chemotherapy for Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: Results from the Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Phase 3 KEYNOTE-522 Study (Annals of surgical oncology, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of adjuvant carboplatin intensified chemotherapy versus standard chemotherapy on survival in women with high risk, early stage, triple negative breast cancer (CITRINE): randomised, open label phase 3 trial (BMJ (Clinical research ed.), 2025)

8 trials 2 Trial pubs
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT04591431 — The Rome Trial From Histology to Target: the Road to Personalize Target Therapy and Immunotherapy (UNKNOWN) PHASE2
  • NCT03805399 — FUSCC Refractory TNBC Umbrella (FUTURE) (UNKNOWN) PHASE1, PHASE2
  • NCT02520063 — Combination of Letrozole, Everolimus and TRC105 in Postmenopausal Women With Hormone-Receptor Positive and Her2 Negative Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE1, PHASE2
  • NCT01088893 — Everolimus in Breast Cancer Patients After Pre-operative Chemotherapy (UNKNOWN) PHASE2
  • NCT03659136 — The XENERA™ 1 Study Tests Xentuzumab in Combination With Everolimus and Exemestane in Women With Hormone Receptor Positive and HER2-negative Breast Cancer That Has Spread (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT05983107 — Chidamide/Everolimus for PIK3CA Wild-type/Mutant HR+/HER2- Advanced Breast Cancer (RECRUITING) PHASE2
  • NCT01111825 — Temsirolimus Plus Neratinib for Patients With Metastatic HER2-Amplified or Triple Negative Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE1, PHASE2
  • NCT02809404 — Dried Blood SPOT Analysis of Everolimus in Cancer Patients (SPOT-study) (COMPLETED)

Published literature

  • Systematic review mTOR inhibitors in breast cancer: a systematic review (Gynecologic oncology, 2012)
  • Clinical trial publication Sirolimus and trastuzumab combination therapy for HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer after progression on prior trastuzumab therapy (Breast cancer research and treatment, 2015)
  • Clinical trial publication Phase I, pharmacokinetic study of temsirolimus administered orally to patients with advanced cancer (Investigational new drugs, 2010)

View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT05435911 — Comparison of Recovery From General Anesthesia in Remimazolam With Flumazenil vs. Propofol (COMPLETED) NA

Published literature

  • Clinical trial publication Effects of remimazolam vs. an etomidate-propofol mixture on postoperative cognitive function in elderly female patients undergoing radical mastectomy for breast cancer: a randomized controlled trial (Frontiers in medicine, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Comparison of recovery profiles between total intravenous anaesthesia with propofol or remimazolam reversed with flumazenil in patients undergoing breast surgery: A randomised controlled trial (European journal of anaesthesiology, 2024)

  • Systematic review Multiple-treatments meta-analysis of chemotherapy and targeted therapies in advanced breast cancer (Journal of the National Cancer Institute, 2008)
  • Systematic review Time to full publication of studies of anti-cancer medicines for breast cancer and the potential for publication bias: a short systematic review (Health technology assessment (Winchester, England), 2008)
  • Clinical trial publication A phase II randomized trial of gemcitabine plus cisplatin (GP) versus gemcitabine plus carboplatin (GC) as the first-line treatment of patients with metastatic triple-negative breast cancer (ESMO open, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Datopotamab deruxtecan versus chemotherapy in previously treated inoperable/metastatic hormone receptor-positive, HER2-negative breast cancer: final overall survival analysis of the phase III TROPION-Breast01 study (Annals of oncology : official journal of the European Society for Medical Oncology, 2026)

Registry trials

  • NCT01196455 — Capecitabine and Mitomycin C in Treatment of Patients With Metastatic Breast Cancer (UNKNOWN) PHASE2
  • NCT02299999 — SAFIR02_Breast - Efficacy of Genome Analysis as a Therapeutic Decision Tool for Patients With Metastatic Breast Cancer (ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING) PHASE2
  • NCT01091168 — Trial of Vinflunine Versus Alkylating Agent in Metastatic Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00003893 — Adjuvant Chemotherapy Plus Radiation Therapy in Treating Women With Early-Stage Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE3

Published literature

  • Systematic review A rapid and systematic review of the effectiveness and cost-effectiveness of the taxanes used in the treatment of advanced breast and ovarian cancer (Health technology assessment (Winchester, England), 2000)
  • Clinical trial publication Role of Mitomycin C in Preventing Capsular Contracture in Implant-Based Reconstructive Breast Surgery: A Randomized Controlled Trial (Plastic and reconstructive surgery, 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication Mitomycin C in combination with vinorelbine in anthracycline- and/or taxane-pretreated patients with metastatic breast cancer (Onkologie, 2012)
  • Clinical trial publication Low expression levels of ATM may substitute for CHEK2 /TP53 mutations predicting resistance towards anthracycline and mitomycin chemotherapy in breast cancer (Breast cancer research : BCR, 2012)
  • Clinical trial publication Combination of capecitabine and mitomycin C as first-line treatment in patients with metastatic breast cancer (Neoplasma, 2011)
  • Clinical trial publication Transarterial chemoembolization (TACE) with mitomycin C and gemcitabine for liver metastases in breast cancer (European radiology, 2010)
  • Randomized controlled trial Randomized phase II trial on mitomycin-C/cisplatin +/- KLT in heavily pretreated advanced breast cancer (The American journal of Chinese medicine, 2008)
  • Randomized controlled trial Good clinical response of breast cancers to neoadjuvant chemoendocrine therapy is associated with improved overall survival (Annals of oncology : official journal of the European Society for Medical Oncology, 2005)

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Efficacy and safety of first-line treatment for metastatic triple-negative breast cancer: A network meta-analysis (Cancer pathogenesis and therapy, 2024)
  • Cochrane review PARP Inhibitors for the Treatment of BRCA1/2-Mutated Metastatic Breast Cancer: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis (Hematology/oncology and stem cell therapy, 2023)
  • Cochrane review Cost-effectiveness of PARP inhibitors in malignancies: A systematic review (PloS one, 2022)
  • Cochrane review Comparative Efficacy and safety of new targeted therapies and immunotherapies for metastatic triple negative breast cancer: a network meta-analysis (Expert opinion on drug safety, 2023)
  • Cochrane review Economic and Humanistic Burden of Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: A Systematic Literature Review (PharmacoEconomics, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis Neurological toxicities with poly (ADP-ribose) polymerase inhibitors in cancer patients: a systematic review and meta-analysis (Journal of chemotherapy (Florence, Italy), 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Poly (ADP-ribose) Polymerase Inhibitors Have Comparable Efficacy with Platinum Chemotherapy in Patients with BRCA-positive Metastatic Castration-resistant Prostate Cancer. A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis (European urology oncology, 2024)
  • Meta-analysis PARP-inhibitors for BRCA1/2-related advanced HER2-negative breast cancer: A meta-analysis and GRADE recommendations by the Italian Association of Medical Oncology (Breast (Edinburgh, Scotland), 2022)
  • Meta-analysis Multiple Bayesian network meta-analyses to establish therapeutic algorithms for metastatic triple negative breast cancer (Cancer treatment reviews, 2022)
  • Systematic review Targeted Treatment of Metastatic Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: A Systematic Review (The breast journal, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication A phase 1 study of ASTX727 plus talazoparib in patients with triple-negative or hormone resistant/HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer (Cancer, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Resistance to neoadjuvant talazoparib in triple-negative breast cancer by BRN2-induced ATR/STAT3 pathways or SHLD2 subclone expansion (Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America, 2026)

  • Clinical trial publication Datopotamab deruxtecan versus chemotherapy in previously treated inoperable/metastatic hormone receptor-positive, HER2-negative breast cancer: final overall survival analysis of the phase III TROPION-Breast01 study (Annals of oncology : official journal of the European Society for Medical Oncology, 2026)

Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.
